title: ControllerCraft
description: Modpack that adds the BARE minimum to run things such as the steam deck and/or controllers with minecraft. (NOTE: you will have to bind your controllers your self)
body:
I see a lot of steam deck/controller minecraft modpacks, but none of them just included the needed stuff (they usually add optimizations, or other stuff.) This modpack just gives the bare minimum needed to run Minecraft on the steam deck, or any controller to be fair. Because of this minimal structure, you can add other mods to this pack and they should work fine.

So far, the only mods that are included are:

Fabric API - Needed as a core API for many fabric mods, including MidnightControls.

MidnightControls - Adds built-in controller support to Minecraft

No Chat Reports - Strips cryptographic signatures from your chat messages. (Read the Reasoning section HERE: https://github.com/Aizistral-Studios/No-Chat-Reports at the very bottom of the README.)

FAQ:

Q: Why not use Quilt/Is this modpack compatible with Quilt?

A: I am more used to Fabric, and Quilt is still in a beta compared to Fabric, so I picked Fabric for stability, and also because mostly everything works on Fabric. *However*, This modpack can run perfectly fine on Quilt, so in theory, yes. This modpack is compatible with Quilt.

Q: Can you add [Insert Optimization or QOL mod here]

A: Read the description and/or summary. This mod only includes the BARE minimal to have a good experience with controllers in Minecraft. Not a general good experience in Minecraft.

Q: Forge version?

A: Yes! I have decided to use a different mod(s) for controller support on forge!


If you are having any issues, instead of contacting me contact the mod developers or send a issue request on their github. Here is a list for the issue pages of the mod developers's mods.

Fabric API - https://github.com/FabricMC/fabric/issues

MidnightControls - https://github.com/TeamMidnightDust/MidnightControls/issues

No Chat Reports - https://github.com/Aizistral-Studios/No-Chat-Reports/issues
